author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

Implemented the "uniqueness across ALL statuses" policy
(option 1 from the ticket).

- `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs` — renamed
  `find_open_slug_collision` → `find_slug_collision` and
  dropped the status filter; one O(1) HashMap lookup now
  catches any holder regardless of status. Updated the
  `Error::SlugCollision` doc and `Display` message (`open
  issue` → `issue`), the `create_issue` doc, and the
  `resolve` doc to call out the v2.6 behavior and the
  pre-v2.6 backwards-compat shape.
- `crates/jjf-storage/src/cache.rs` — extended `slug_index`
  to carry every issue's slug; active holders inserted
  first so legacy pre-v2.6 duplicate slugs still resolve to
  the active issue.
- `crates/jjf/src/main.rs` — CLI-side `SlugCollision`
  variant doc + Display message aligned with storage.
- `docs/storage-format.md` — bumped v2.5 → v2.6 with a
  changelog entry; rewrote §3.4 with the new rule and the
  backwards-compat note.
- `docs/cli-json.md` — slug_collision example envelope
  message refreshed.

Tests added:
- `slug_uniqueness_scope_spans_all_statuses_including_closed`
  (replaces the now-incorrect `..._is_open_only`).
- `slug_uniqueness_blocks_against_blocked_holder`
  (regression guard for the active-status path).
- `resolve_still_finds_closed_issue_by_slug` (resolver
  behavior unchanged).
- `snapshot_slug_index_carries_closed_issues` (cache unit).
- CLI: `new_slug_collision_against_closed_holder_is_exit_two`
  asserts envelope kind/details + exit 2.

`cargo nextest run --workspace` green: 485 passed.

Manual repro in
`/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-myers-p-jjforge/.../scratchpad/qa/slug-policy-verify/`
creates "First" with slug `shared`, closes it, then
re-attempts `jjf new --slug shared` — exits 2 with
`{"kind":"slug_collision","details":{"slug":"shared","conflicts_with":"ccfe00e"}}`.

# Recommendation

Closing as fixed. Backwards-compat path documented (write
boundary enforces v2.6; read path tolerates legacy
duplicates and surfaces the active holder).

# Confidence

High. Full test suite green; manual repro confirms the
exact failure mode in the ticket is now prevented. The
backwards-compat path is exercised implicitly by the
existing `from_parts_with_kind` ordering (active inserted
first) and explicitly covered in the new cache unit test.

# Open follow-ups

- A `--allow-slug-recycle` escape hatch on `jjf new` would
  let a deliberate operator re-claim a closed issue's
  slug. Not implemented; file as ergonomics ticket if
  needed.
- Pre-v2.6 repos with existing open/closed slug duplicates
  will surface the active holder via `resolve`; a one-shot
  audit verb (`jjf slug doctor`) could enumerate them. Not
  filed.
